scope_note: This is a same-axis weak defense comparator on the adapted full-ReAct AgentPoison MiniMax setup. It keeps the StrategyQA fullreact manifest, upstream DPR retrieval path, and trigger_question_only adversarial search context. It rewrites unsafe retrieved memory into a safe view instead of quarantining it. It must not be described as full official AgentPoison reproduction.
comparison_question: On the fixed MiniMax adapted AgentPoison full-ReAct comparator, does a safe-view rewrite-only weak defense contain exposed poisoned retrieval and attack manifestation, and how does it compare with FlowFence-Lite quarantine plus canonical ReAct action writeback?

interpretation.observation: The rewrite-only weak comparator reduced exposed poisoned retrieval and attack manifestation to zero in all three runs, without using quarantine. Raw poisoned retrieval remained non-zero with mean 0.4533, close to the no-defense and FlowFence-Lite quarantine-actioncanon groups in the existing main matrix.
interpretation.safety: This shows that safe-view rewriting is also sufficient to prevent exposed poisoned retrieval on this adapted axis when the same detector identifies the poisoned retrieval events.
interpretation.utility: Clean utility mean was 0.2933, below quarantine-actioncanon mean 0.36 and quarantine-only mean 0.3067. Attacked utility mean was 0.4933, higher than the other groups, but utility is noisy under MiniMax full-ReAct and should not be framed as a robust improvement.
interpretation.method_positioning: The weak comparator strengthens the paper by adding a non-quarantine same-axis baseline. It also narrows the method claim: the current evidence supports detector-mediated retrieval sanitization/containment broadly, while quarantine-actioncanon remains the selected method because it has stronger clean utility than rewrite-only and a clearer containment semantics for untrusted memory.
